facts_disclosed: Switched to women-only gym in Rusholme

turns.content: so I've made a gym change recently and honestly it's been life changing. needed to tell someone loool
Ha, I love that energy — what kind of change are we talking? New routine, new gym, new approach entirely?

new gym. I found this sisters-only gym in Rusholme and wallah I wish I'd switched ages ago. it's women only so I can actually work out properly without my hijab on. you don't realise how much of a difference that makes until you experience it

Oh that's massive — being able to work out without worrying about your hijab must change the whole experience, especially for anything high-intensity. Rusholme makes sense too, big community there. What kind of workouts are you doing now that feel different?

cardio is the biggest one. running on the treadmill with hijab is just uncomfortable. it gets hot, you have to keep adjusting it, the pins dig in. now I can just put my hair up and go. I ran 5k for the first time last week without stopping and I genuinely think it's cos I wasn't fighting my scarf the whole time

A 5K without stopping is a proper milestone — congrats on that! And yeah, the thermoregulation thing alone would make a huge difference for running. Your head and neck are major heat-release points, so having those uncovered during intense cardio is genuinely more efficient, not just more comfortable.

Are you following any kind of training plan, or just going by feel?
honestly just going by feel right now. I go like 3 or 4 times a week. usually after work. I do a mix of cardio and some weights but I'm not following a proper programme. should I be?

If you're going 3-4 times a week consistently, that's a great foundation. A loose structure could help you progress faster without making it feel like a chore. Something simple like:

- **2 days** focused on strength (upper/lower split or full body)
- **1-2 days** cardio-focused (building on that 5K progress)

You don't need a rigid programme at this stage — even just having a rough plan of "today is a weights day" vs "today is a running day" keeps you from defaulting to the same thing every time. Are you enjoying the weights side of things, or is cardio more your vibe?

I'm getting into weights tbh. theres a few regulars at the gym who've been showing me things. like one of the trainers there showed me proper deadlift form last week and I was dead sore for 3 days but in a good way loool

That post-deadlift soreness hits different — if your hamstrings and lower back were talking to you for three days, you did it right! Good that someone showed you proper form too, deadlifts are one of those lifts where technique matters a lot for avoiding injury.

Having a community at the gym makes such a difference for consistency. Way easier to show up when you know people there and you're learning from them. Are the trainers included in the membership or do you pay extra for that?

it's a pretty small gym so it's more informal than that. the owner is there most days and she just helps people out. the vibe is completely different from a big chain gym. everyone knows each other, it's really welcoming. some of the aunties there are absolutely jacked btw it's mad

The jacked aunties are the best part of any community gym — they've been at it for years and they will humble you with their deadlift numbers. That kind of environment is gold for actually sticking with it long-term. The big chains have the equipment but the culture of a small gym where the owner is hands-on is hard to beat.

Is it reasonably priced compared to what you were paying before?

it's actually cheaper which is wild. like £25 a month. my old gym was £30 and it was bare crowded, you'd have to queue for machines during peak hours. this place is never rammed cos it's smaller and the membership is capped

£25 a month with capped membership and personal attention from the owner? That's genuinely great value. No machine queues, better community, more comfortable environment, and cheaper — that's a clean sweep. Makes sense why you wish you'd found it sooner.

Sounds like the main thing holding you back before was the environment rather than the motivation. Now that's sorted, you might find your progress accelerates quite a bit.

100%. like I always wanted to be consistent but it was hard to get motivated when the gym felt like a chore. now I actually look forward to going which is mad for me cos I used to hate exercise. my friend thinks I've been body snatched loool

Ha — when the right environment clicks, it doesn't feel like discipline anymore, it just feels like something you do. That's the difference between forcing yourself to go and actually wanting to. Your friend will get used to the new you.

Keep riding that momentum. And keep learning from the jacked aunties — they clearly know what they're doing.
wallah they do. right I need to go actually, got a session booked for this evening and I need to eat something first. thanks for the chat!
Get a good meal in and enjoy the session! Glad you found a place that works for you.
